D2L Brightspace prospecting run · 2026-05-10

Seller-Side Action Board

Conservative pass on the uploaded prospect list. Work Now is intentionally tight. Light Research holds plausible names that still need remit or trigger proof. Suppress keeps weak-fit rows out of Pat's queue.

260Total prospects
17Work Now
130Light Research
113Suppress

Executive Summary

Accounts reviewed 57Top theme Association / member organizationEmails present 203

The best pockets in this list are association/member organizations, credentialing/CE names, and a smaller workforce/training cluster where the title plus account context is good enough to work now. The main limit is thin why-now evidence. Most rows have org and title, but not enough program-level proof to write a sharper opener without one more check.

Segment themes: Association / member organization: 80; Poor fit / suppress: 50; Healthcare / clinical education: 43; Corporate L&D / workforce development: 42; Training provider / extended enterprise: 34; Credentialing / certification / CE: 11

Evidence gaps: 57 rows are missing direct email. 113 rows were held back to avoid padding the queue with weak-fit names.

0 prospects marked Sent or Connected.

Manager Readout

Work this week should start with the highest-score credentialing, association education, and healthcare education names that already have direct email and a plausible learning remit. Hold back the broad corporate and school-service-center rows unless a cleaner learning owner or trigger turns up.

The safest story theme is not "replace your LMS." It is compare-notes language around CE delivery, member learning, external training scale, reporting, and operational lift.

Data quality is good enough to move, but not broad enough to justify a high-volume blast. This run is better used as a sharp first-pass queue than as a mass cadence list.

Work Now76

Helen Horner

Senior Director, Meetings and Education Programs · Association Of Equipment Manufacturers

Association / member organizationhhorner@aem.orgstrong evidencePat Ducoffe
Fit91
Urgency48
Persona84
Evidence68

Verified Facts

Title supplied: Senior Director, Meetings and Education Programs; Work email supplied; Account supplied: Association Of Equipment Manufacturers

Likely Pain

member education, CE participation, and cleaner reporting across programs

What To Check First

Validate current priority and tailor the opener lightly.

Unknowns

Current priority and timing still need confirmation.

Helen - I spend a lot of time with teams working through member education, CE participation, and cleaner reporting across programs. Your role at Association Of Equipment Manufacturers seemed close to that world, so I thought it could be worth connecting.
Subject line
Quick thought on Association Of Equipment Manufacturers's learning programs
Email body
Hi Helen,

I spend a lot of time with teams working through member education, CE participation, and cleaner reporting across programs. Your role at Association Of Equipment Manufacturers looked pretty close to that world, so I wanted to reach out.

Curious how you're thinking about that this year, especially if there is any push around program scale, CE, reporting, or learner experience. Happy to compare notes if useful.

Best,
How are you thinking about scaling learning while keeping the learner experience useful and the reporting clean?
Makes sense. I would not frame this as a rip-and-replace conversation. I would be more curious where the current setup is helping and where the team still has manual workarounds around CE, reporting, member data, or learner engagement.

Brightspace Angle

Compare notes on member learning, CE, and non-dues education delivery without assuming a platform change is needed.

Why Now

Multiple education/training titles plus reachable contacts in the account.

Do Not Claim

Do not claim urgency, initiative ownership, dissatisfaction with current tools, or budget without more proof.

Next Move

Send first touch

Not touched yet
Work Now76

Colette - I spend a lot of time with teams working through repeatable certification or CE delivery, completion tracking, and proof of learning. Your role at National Board of Medical Examiners seemed close to that world, so I thought it could be worth connecting.
Subject line
Quick thought on National Board of Medical Examiners's learning programs
Email body
Hi Colette,

I spend a lot of time with teams working through repeatable certification or CE delivery, completion tracking, and proof of learning. Your role at National Board of Medical Examiners looked pretty close to that world, so I wanted to reach out.

Curious how you're thinking about that this year, especially if there is any push around program scale, CE, reporting, or learner experience. Happy to compare notes if useful.

Best,
How are you thinking about scaling learning while keeping the learner experience useful and the reporting clean?
Makes sense. I would not frame this as a rip-and-replace conversation. I would be more curious where the current setup is helping and where the team still has manual workarounds around CE, reporting, member data, or learner engagement.

Brightspace Angle

Use a credentialing and CE operations angle with light proof from D2L's PMI credibility.

Why Now

Credentialing or CE motion is visible in the account and worth a careful first touch.

Do Not Claim

Do not claim urgency, initiative ownership, dissatisfaction with current tools, or budget without more proof.

Subject line
Quick question on Michigan Health & Hospital Association
Email body
Hi Ruthanne,

Your role at Michigan Health & Hospital Association caught my eye. I may be off here, but I was curious whether learning, member education, or training is part of your world this year.

If it is, happy to compare notes on what similar teams are doing. If not, no worries at all.

Best,
Does learning, member education, training, or CE sit anywhere near your priorities this year?
Totally fair. I would not lead with replacing anything. I would mostly want to understand whether the current setup is doing the job or whether there are still workarounds around reporting, learner experience, or program ops.

Brightspace Angle

Lead with reliable education delivery, CE workflows, and reporting rather than a broad LMS pitch.

Why Now

Healthcare or clinical education context is visible, but the current program motion still needs validation.

Do Not Claim

Do not claim urgency, initiative ownership, dissatisfaction with current tools, or budget without more proof.

Hi Jennifer,

Your role at Training Camp caught my eye. I may be off here, but I was curious whether learning, member education, or training is part of your world this year.

If it is, happy to compare notes on what similar teams are doing. If not, no worries at all.

Best,
Does learning, member education, training, or CE sit anywhere near your priorities this year?
Totally fair. I would not lead with replacing anything. I would mostly want to understand whether the current setup is doing the job or whether there are still workarounds around reporting, learner experience, or program ops.

Brightspace Angle

Use an external training scale and learner experience angle, especially if programs reach customers, members, or partners.

Why Now

Do Not Claim

Do not claim urgency, initiative ownership, dissatisfaction with current tools, or budget without more proof.

Quick question on Turning Technologies - DO NOT USE - Go to Echo360
Email body
Hi ASC,

Your role at Turning Technologies - DO NOT USE - Go to Echo360 caught my eye. I may be off here, but I was curious whether learning, member education, or training is part of your world this year.

If it is, happy to compare notes on what similar teams are doing. If not, no worries at all.

Best,
Does learning, member education, training, or CE sit anywhere near your priorities this year?
Totally fair. I would not lead with replacing anything. I would mostly want to understand whether the current setup is doing the job or whether there are still workarounds around reporting, learner experience, or program ops.

Brightspace Angle

Do not position Brightspace until a real training or education motion is proven.

What To Do Next

  1. Helen Horner at Association Of Equipment Manufacturers: Send first touch. Why now: Multiple education/training titles plus reachable contacts in the account.
  2. Alicia Steindorf at Credit Union National Association: Send first touch. Why now: Multiple education/training titles plus reachable contacts in the account.
  3. Ariel Bilskey at Credit Union National Association: Send first touch. Why now: Multiple education/training titles plus reachable contacts in the account.

  8. Joseph Skrajewski at Hazelden Foundation: Send first touch. Why now: Multiple education/training titles plus reachable contacts in the account.

Weekly Plan

Monday: Send first touches to the highest-score Work Now names.

Tuesday: Tighten personalization for the top Light Research names.

Wednesday: Call or lightly email the best association and credentialing contacts.

Thursday: Send follow-ups and LinkedIn touches to the engaged names.

Friday: Review replies, suppress weak rows, and set up next week’s queue.

Suppression logic

Suppress rows were kept out of active work because the export alone did not show enough Brightspace fit, enough reachability, or enough remit proof. That includes clear placeholders like the Echo360 row, thin corporate rows with no learning owner, and school-service-center style rows that would need a different motion.
